description: Profile following and PID autotune features for the rustRoast coffee roaster dashboard. The backend is ~95% complete for both features — work is primarily frontend SvelteKit components with minor backend additions. Uses SvelteKit 2 with Svelte 5 runes ($state, $derived, $effect), Tailwind CSS 4 with OKLch dark theme tokens, Apache ECharts (tree-shaken via echarts/core), lucide-svelte icons. Existing infrastructure: API client at $lib/api/client.ts, telemetry WebSocket store at $lib/stores/telemetry.ts, Chart.svelte wrapper, TelemetryChart.svelte, CurrentReadings.svelte, SessionControls.svelte, PidControls.svelte. Profile CRUD endpoints exist (GET/POST /api/profiles, GET/DELETE /api/profiles/:id, POST /api/profiles/import/artisan). Autotune endpoints exist (POST /api/roaster/:device_id/autotune/start|stop|apply, GET status/results). WebSocket at /ws/telemetry already forwards autotune events. ESP32 firmware implements full relay feedback Ziegler-Nichols autotune. CRITICAL: All files MUST be .svelte or .ts — no .tsx, no React. Svelte 5 rune stores use .svelte.ts extension. Follow existing patterns from dashboard components.

userStories.title: Add profile create/update backend support
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the backend to support creating profiles via the frontend API client and updating existing profiles so the profile designer has complete CRUD operations.
acceptanceCriteria: profiles.create() method added to frontend API client ($lib/api/client.ts) calling POST /api/profiles with CreateProfileRequest body (name, description, charge_temp, target_end_temp, target_total_time, points[])
PUT /api/profiles/:id endpoint added to backend main.rs for updating profile metadata and replacing all points
Update service method in session_service replaces all profile points atomically: DELETE old points + INSERT new in a SQLx transaction
profiles.update() method added to frontend API client calling PUT /api/profiles/:id
cargo test passes (add test for profile update round-trip)
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
npm run check passes with zero errors
userStories.notes: The backend already has create_profile, list_profiles, get_profile_with_points, delete_profile, import_artisan_profile in session_service (crates/server/src/services/sessions.rs). Profile routes are in main.rs around line 1361. CreateProfileRequest model exists in models.rs. The DB tables roast_profiles and profile_points exist with FK cascade delete. For the update endpoint, model it after api_create_profile but accept the profile ID from the path and do an UPDATE on metadata + DELETE/INSERT on points within a transaction. The frontend API client is at apps/dashboard/src/lib/api/client.ts — the profiles object is missing create() (backend endpoint exists). Add update() once the backend endpoint is ready.

userStories.title: Add global settings table and API
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a key-value settings table in the database so user preferences like lookahead time persist across sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: settings table created in the init_db function in main.rs: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS settings (key TEXT PRIMARY KEY, value TEXT NOT NULL, updated_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')))
Default setting seeded: INSERT OR IGNORE INTO settings (key, value) VALUES ('profile_lookahead_seconds', '20')
GET /api/settings endpoint returns all settings as a JSON object { key: value, ... }
PUT /api/settings/:key endpoint sets a single setting value (upsert pattern)
GET /api/settings is a public endpoint (no auth required)
PUT /api/settings/:key is a protected endpoint (requires auth)
Frontend API client has settings.get() returning Promise<Record<string, string>> and settings.set(key, value) returning Promise<void>
cargo test passes
npm run check passes with zero errors
userStories.notes: Add the table in init_db alongside other CREATE TABLE statements in main.rs. For the endpoints, add them inline in main.rs following the pattern of other simple handlers. Settings GET returns all rows as { key: value } JSON object using a simple SELECT query. Settings PUT uses INSERT OR REPLACE (SQLite upsert). Frontend API client methods go in the existing $lib/api/client.ts file — add a new 'settings' export object similar to the profiles object.

userStories.title: Create profile store with Svelte 5 runes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a centralized profile store so multiple components (chart, readings, controls) can access the active profile and following state.
acceptanceCriteria: New file $lib/stores/profile.svelte.ts using Svelte 5 $state runes
Exports reactive state: activeProfile (ProfileWithPoints | null), isFollowing (boolean), lastSentSetpoint (number | null)
Export functions: loadProfile(id: string), unloadProfile(), startFollowing(), stopFollowing()
loadProfile fetches profile via profiles.get(id) and sets activeProfile
unloadProfile clears activeProfile and stops following
interpolateTarget(elapsedMs: number, lookaheadMs: number) pure function: linear interpolation between sorted profile points, holds first/last point values at boundaries
interpolateTarget exported separately for unit testing
npm run check passes with zero errors
userStories.notes: Use .svelte.ts extension to enable runes outside components. Pattern: let activeProfile = $state<ProfileWithPoints | null>(null). Export as getter functions or use the module-level $state pattern. The interpolation function should be pure (no side effects) and take elapsed time in milliseconds + lookahead in milliseconds, returning target temperature. Profile points have time_seconds and target_temp fields — convert to milliseconds for chart compatibility. Sort points by time_seconds before interpolating.

userStories.title: Create profile designer with visual chart editor
userStories.description: As a roaster, I want to create and edit roast profiles by placing points on a chart so I can design temperature curves visually.
acceptanceCriteria: New ProfileDesigner.svelte component at $lib/components/ProfileDesigner.svelte
ECharts scatter+line chart showing profile points (X: time in seconds, Y: temperature in °C)
Click on chart area to add a new point at that time/temperature position using ECharts click event
Click existing point to select it and show editable input fields for time_seconds, target_temp, fan_speed
Delete selected point with a delete button or keyboard Delete key
Points connected with lines and sorted by time_seconds automatically
Table view toggle: button switches between chart view and editable data table showing all points
Profile metadata inputs: name (required), description, charge_temp, target_end_temp
Save button calls profiles.create() for new or profiles.update() for existing profiles
Cancel button navigates back to /profiles without saving
New route files: src/routes/profiles/new/+page.svelte and src/routes/profiles/[id]/edit/+page.svelte that render ProfileDesigner
npm run check passes with zero errors
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Use the existing Chart.svelte wrapper component. For click-to-add, listen to the ECharts 'click' event on the chart instance. Use ECharts scatter series for draggable points overlaid on a line series. For the table view, use a simple HTML table with input fields bound to point data via $state. The designer should accept an optional profileId prop — if provided, load existing profile data for editing. Route /profiles/new renders ProfileDesigner with no initial data. Route /profiles/[id]/edit loads profile via a +page.ts load function and passes data to ProfileDesigner. Use goto('/profiles') from $app/navigation on cancel/save. Chart styling should match existing TelemetryChart colors (dark theme, amber accents).

userStories.title: Add profile overlay to telemetry chart
userStories.description: As a roaster, I want to see the loaded profile as a reference curve on the real-time telemetry chart so I can visually track how well I'm following the target profile.
acceptanceCriteria: When activeProfile is set in the profile store, TelemetryChart renders an additional 'Profile' series
Profile series is a dashed purple line (#a855f7) with lineStyle: { type: 'dashed', width: 2 }
Profile curve X-axis uses elapsed milliseconds (same as telemetry data) — convert profile points from seconds to ms
Profile series shows in the chart legend labeled 'Profile'
Loading/unloading a profile does not clear telemetry history or reset the chart
Profile overlay works correctly alongside BT, ET, RoR, Heater, and Fan series
npm run check passes with zero errors
userStories.notes: Import the profile store in TelemetryChart.svelte. In the $derived.by chart option computation, check if activeProfile has points. If so, add a new series to the series array: { name: 'Profile', type: 'line', data: points.map(p => [p.time_seconds * 1000, p.target_temp]), itemStyle: { color: '#a855f7' }, lineStyle: { type: 'dashed', width: 2 }, showSymbol: false }. The profile uses yAxisIndex: 0 (temperature axis, same as BT/ET). No markLine needed — it's a full series.

userStories.title: Implement profile following (auto setpoint control)
userStories.description: As a roaster, I want the system to automatically set temperature targets from the loaded profile when in auto mode so that the PID controller follows the profile curve.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Follow Profile' toggle button added to SessionControls.svelte or near the session control area
Button only enabled when: a profile is loaded AND a session is active (started, not paused)
When enabled, uses $effect with setInterval(1000) to run every second
Each tick: interpolates target temp at (current_elapsed_ms + lookahead_ms) using profile store interpolateTarget()
Sends POST /api/roaster/:device_id/control/setpoint only when |new_target - lastSentSetpoint| >= 1.0°C (hysteresis)
Automatically calls control.setMode(deviceId, 'auto') when following starts
Following stops when: session ends, profile is unloaded, or user clicks toggle off
Lookahead value read from settings store (profile_lookahead_seconds, default 20s)
npm run check passes with zero errors
userStories.notes: The follow logic lives in a $effect in a component (e.g., SessionControls or a new FollowProfile.svelte helper component). Use the telemetry store to get current elapsed time from the session start. The control API is at $lib/api/client.ts: control.setSetpoint(deviceId, value). The profile store's startFollowing/stopFollowing manage the isFollowing boolean. The setInterval runs inside a $effect cleanup pattern: $effect(() => { if (!isFollowing) return; const id = setInterval(..., 1000); return () => clearInterval(id); }). Read settings on mount via settings.get() and extract profile_lookahead_seconds.

userStories.title: Show profile temperature delta in current readings
userStories.description: As a roaster, I want to see the difference between actual bean temperature and the profile target so I can quickly assess if the roast is ahead or behind the curve.
acceptanceCriteria: CurrentReadings.svelte shows a 'Delta' value when a profile is loaded and a session is active
Delta = actual BT - profile target BT at current elapsed time (positive = ahead/hotter, negative = behind/cooler)
Display format: '+2.3°C' or '-1.5°C' with sign always shown
Color coding: text-green-400 when |delta| <= 2°C, text-amber-400 when 2-5°C, text-red-400 when > 5°C
Delta updates with each telemetry tick (real-time)
Delta row hidden when no profile is loaded or no active session
npm run check passes with zero errors
userStories.notes: Import the profile store into CurrentReadings.svelte. Use $derived to compute the delta: get current BT from telemetry, get current elapsed time, call interpolateTarget(elapsedMs, 0) (no lookahead for display — show actual delta, not lookahead delta). The CurrentReadings component already has a readings grid pattern — add a new entry conditionally rendered with {#if activeProfile && elapsedMs > 0}. Use $derived for the color class based on Math.abs(delta).

userStories.title: Add profile selector to session creation
userStories.description: As a roaster, I want to select a profile when creating a new roast session so the profile is automatically loaded for following.
acceptanceCriteria: Profile dropdown added to the session creation flow (wherever sessions are created — check SessionControls.svelte or the dashboard page)
Dropdown shows profile list with name and basic metadata (charge temp, duration)
Selected profile_id included in POST /api/sessions request body
When a session with a profile is started, the profile store automatically loads the associated profile
User can start a session without selecting a profile (optional field)
npm run check passes with zero errors
userStories.notes: The CreateSessionRequest in the API client accepts an optional profile_id field — check models.rs for the backend model. The sessions.create() function in client.ts sends the request. Profile list can be fetched via profiles.list(). When a session starts and has a profile_id, call profileStore.loadProfile(profileId). Check how session creation currently works — it may be in SessionControls.svelte or the main dashboard page. The profile dropdown should use the same select styling as DeviceSelector (bg-card, border-border, text-foreground).

userStories.title: Integrate Artisan import into profile designer
userStories.description: As a roaster, I want to import an Artisan .alog file and review/edit the imported points in the profile designer before saving.
acceptanceCriteria: Import button on the profiles page opens a file picker for .alog/.json files
After selecting a file, navigates to /profiles/new with imported data passed via URL state or a temporary store
Imported profile opens in ProfileDesigner with points visible on the chart for review
User can edit imported points before saving (same editing capabilities as manual creation)
Uses existing POST /api/profiles/import/artisan endpoint to parse the file, then loads result into designer
Error messages shown for invalid or unparseable files using notifications store
npm run check passes with zero errors
userStories.notes: The current profiles page already has an import flow that calls profiles.importArtisan() and saves directly. Change the flow to: (1) call importArtisan to parse and save, (2) redirect to /profiles/[newId]/edit to open in designer. OR: create a client-side parser that reads the .alog JSON and populates the designer without saving first. The simpler approach is to import via API (which saves), then redirect to the edit page. The existing import code is in src/routes/profiles/+page.svelte handleImport().

userStories.title: Create autotune store for state management
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a centralized autotune store so multiple components can access autotune status and results consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: New file $lib/stores/autotune.svelte.ts using Svelte 5 $state runes
Exports reactive state: status (phase string, step count, progress %), results (Kp, Ki, Kd numbers or null), isAutotuning (boolean), targetTemp (number | null)
Derives state from WebSocket autotune events in the telemetry stream (subscribe to telemetry store messages)
Parses autotune event types: status updates set phase/step/progress, results updates set Kp/Ki/Kd
On initialization, fetches latest status via GET /api/roaster/:device_id/autotune/status/latest to handle page refresh during active autotune
Export functions: startAutotune(deviceId, targetTemp), stopAutotune(deviceId), applyResults(deviceId), dismissResults()
dismissResults() clears results state
npm run check passes with zero errors
userStories.notes: The WebSocket at /ws/telemetry forwards autotune events. In main.rs telemetry_ws_loop, autotune messages are sent as JSON: { autotune: { type: 'status'|'results', data: ... } }. The store should subscribe to the telemetry WebSocket messages and filter for autotune events. Use the control API for start/stop/apply: the autotune endpoints are on the existing API client pattern but not yet in client.ts — add autotune.start(deviceId, { target_temp }), autotune.stop(deviceId), autotune.apply(deviceId). For the status fetch on init, call fetch directly or add to the API client.

userStories.title: Create autotune control panel
userStories.description: As a roaster, I want a UI panel to start and control PID autotune so I can find optimal PID parameters.
acceptanceCriteria: New AutotunePanel.svelte component at $lib/components/AutotunePanel.svelte
Target temperature number input with range 150-250°C (default 200°C) with step=5
Start Autotune button calls autotuneStore.startAutotune(deviceId, targetTemp)
Stop Autotune button (shown while autotuning) calls autotuneStore.stopAutotune(deviceId)
Panel disabled or shows warning when a roast session is in progress
Panel only functional when a device is selected
Accessible from the settings page in a dedicated 'PID Autotune' section
Uses dark theme styling consistent with other panels (bg-card, border-border)
npm run check passes with zero errors
userStories.notes: Component receives deviceId as a prop. Use $state for targetTemp input. The start button should be disabled while isAutotuning is true, and stop should only show when isAutotuning. Check session status from the telemetry store or session state to prevent starting during a roast. Place on the settings page (src/routes/settings/+page.svelte) as a new section below existing settings content. Use lucide-svelte icons: Play, Square, Zap for start/stop/autotune.

userStories.title: Display autotune progress in real-time
userStories.description: As a roaster, I want to see autotune progress while it's running so I know how far along the relay test is.
acceptanceCriteria: AutotunePanel shows current phase: Heating, Stabilizing, Running, Analyzing, Complete, Error
Phase displayed with appropriate color: amber for heating/stabilizing, green for running, blue for analyzing, green for complete, red for error
Shows step count during Running phase (e.g., 'Step 7 of ~15')
Progress bar with estimated percentage based on step_count / estimated_total (estimate ~15 cycles)
Shows current BT reading and target temperature during autotune
Status updates in real-time from WebSocket events (no polling)
Error state shows descriptive message from the autotune event data
npm run check passes with zero errors
userStories.notes: All state comes from the autotune store. The store's status object has phase, step_count from the WebSocket events. Progress estimate: (step_count / 15) * 100, capped at 95% until ANALYZING phase. Use Tailwind progress bar: div with bg-amber-500 and width percentage via inline style. Phase colors via conditional class: {#if phase === 'RUNNING'}text-green-400{:else if phase === 'ERROR'}text-red-400{/if}. Current BT comes from the telemetry store.

userStories.title: Display autotune results with apply/discard
userStories.description: As a roaster, I want to see recommended PID parameters after autotune and choose to apply or discard them.
acceptanceCriteria: Results section in AutotunePanel shows Kp, Ki, Kd values after autotune completes
Current PID values displayed alongside recommended values for comparison (current values from telemetry: Kp, Ki, Kd fields)
Apply button calls autotuneStore.applyResults(deviceId) which sends POST /api/roaster/:device_id/autotune/apply
Discard button calls autotuneStore.dismissResults() to clear the results display
Success notification shown after applying (via notifications store)
Failure notification shown if apply fails
Results persist in the store until dismissed (survive component re-renders)
On page load, if autotuneStore has no results, fetch via GET /api/roaster/:device_id/autotune/results/latest
npm run check passes with zero errors
userStories.notes: The results object from the autotune event contains Kp, Ki, Kd as numbers. Current PID values come from the latest telemetry (telemetry store: $telemetry.Kp, $telemetry.Ki, $telemetry.Kd). Display as a comparison table: Current | Recommended columns. The apply endpoint sends the PID values to the ESP32 via MQTT. Use the notifications store from $lib/stores/notifications.ts for success/failure messages. Style the results section with bg-green-500/10 border-green-500/30 to indicate success.

userStories.title: Show autotune annotations on telemetry chart
userStories.description: As a roaster, I want to see autotune-related annotations on the telemetry chart during and after a relay test.
acceptanceCriteria: During autotune, horizontal dashed line at target temperature shown on the chart (#f97316 orange)
Autotune target line labeled 'AT Target' in the legend
Autotune target line only shows when isAutotuning is true or results are pending
Existing BT, ET, Heater, Fan, RoR series continue to render normally during autotune
npm run check passes with zero errors
userStories.notes: Import the autotune store in TelemetryChart.svelte. When isAutotuning or results are pending, add a markLine to the BT series (or a separate constant-value series) at the targetTemp value. Use markLine: { data: [{ yAxis: targetTemp, lineStyle: { color: '#f97316', type: 'dashed' }, label: { formatter: 'AT Target', color: '#f97316' } }] }. Alternative: add a separate series with a constant value across the full X range. The simpler markLine approach is preferred.

userStories.title: Add lookahead setting to settings page
userStories.description: As a roaster, I want to configure the profile following lookahead time in settings so I can adjust how far ahead the system anticipates the profile curve.
acceptanceCriteria: Lookahead input field on settings page with descriptive label: 'Profile Lookahead (seconds)'
Help text explaining: 'How far ahead to read the profile curve when following. Higher values smooth out transitions. Default: 20s'
Number input with range 5-60 seconds, step=1
Current value loaded from backend settings API on page load
Changes saved via PUT /api/settings/profile_lookahead_seconds on blur or with a Save button
Default value of 20 shown if no setting exists
npm run check passes with zero errors
userStories.notes: Add to the existing settings page at src/routes/settings/+page.svelte. Load settings via the settings.get() API method in the page's load function or on mount. Use $state for the input value. Save on change using settings.set('profile_lookahead_seconds', String(value)). Follow the same input styling pattern as other settings fields on the page.

userStories.title: Add frontend unit tests for profile and autotune features
userStories.description: As a developer, I want unit tests covering profile interpolation, store state transitions, and hysteresis logic.
acceptanceCriteria: Test file for interpolateTarget function: linear interpolation between two points, extrapolation before first point (returns first temp), extrapolation after last point (returns last temp), exact point match, single-point profile
Test file for profile store: loadProfile sets activeProfile, unloadProfile clears state, startFollowing/stopFollowing toggles isFollowing
Test file for autotune store: status transitions (idle → heating → running → complete), result parsing from event data, dismissResults clears state
Test for hysteresis logic: setpoint only sent when delta >= 1°C
All tests pass with npm test
npm run check passes with zero errors
userStories.notes: Tests go in files alongside source: e.g., $lib/stores/profile.test.ts, $lib/stores/autotune.test.ts. The project uses vitest (see existing tests: $lib/stores/telemetry.test.ts). For testing Svelte 5 rune stores (.svelte.ts files), you may need to test the exported pure functions directly rather than the reactive state. The interpolateTarget function should be a pure exported function specifically for testability. Mock fetch for API calls in store tests.
